description_long: This is a tall, rectangular apartment building with a repetitive, modular facade typical of mid-20th-century urban architecture. The exterior is dominated by concrete and features a series of stacked balconies on both sides, each with a solid parapet and small, regularly spaced windows. The building stands on a set of robust pillars, elevating the ground floor and creating an open space beneath. The facade is symmetrical, with vertical rows of windows and balconies, and the roofline is flat with a slight overhang. The color palette is primarily gray and weathered, giving the structure a utilitarian, Brutalist appearance. The overall design suggests a focus on function and density, suitable for high-occupancy residential use in an urban environment.
description: A tall, gray, concrete apartment building with stacked balconies, small windows, and a symmetrical, utilitarian facade. The structure is elevated on pillars and features a flat roof.
description_view_0: The narrow side of the building is visible, showing the depth of the balconies and the repetitive, stacked design.
description_view_1: The wide front facade is shown, highlighting the symmetrical arrangement of windows and balconies, as well as the supporting pillars at the base.
description_view_2: The opposite narrow side is visible, mirroring the features of view 0 with stacked balconies and a flat roof.
description_view_3: The rear wide facade is shown, similar to the front, with symmetrical windows and balconies, and visible support pillars.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are arranged in vertical columns, evenly spaced and symmetrically aligned across each floor, with each window paired with a balcony.
